activities.name: Quakers Bay Loop lead by Andrew
activities.description: And a glorious plunge afterwards to cool down

activities.name: Glorious Wattamolla Lagoon
activities.description: Water temp: 86F. Seriously a delicious warm bath. Note cliff jumpers

activities.name: Singapore in Sydney
activities.description: It was rather muggy this morning… But beautiful! (With Grace and Gilad)

activities.name: Avoca Beach walk with Chris, Jack, and Paul
activities.description: Showery at times, but beautiful.

activities.name: Walk with Adam on his home loop
Joining Phil for his regular swim at Clovelly
Low tide scramble with Chris and the glorious Sydney sandstone
Sirius Cove to Bradley’s Head and back with Andrew
activities.description: And some operator failure on the watch. It was about 4 miles total)

activities.name: Extra miles not recorded on Sirius Cove run
Home sweet home
activities.description: Photos from earlier today

activities.name: Bridle trails on a foggy morning
activities.description: With Mintu, Anita, Herve, Lauren, and Tamara

activities.name: Wasn’t feeling it this morning
activities.description: Apologies for abandoning the team!

activities.name: Linking Lakes
activities.description: Listening to “Hail Mary” by Andy Weir

activities.name: Trying out our x-country skies at Hyak
activities.description: Verdict: skis and boot excellent. Conditions at Hyak right now so-so.

activities.name: Northern "B" with the gang
activities.description: We were all running smoothly… even me!

activities.name: Day 1 of Antigua to Lake Atitlán
activities.description: cloud forest and farms at 6-8000’. Amazing day. Camping above a village on the flanks of Acatenango

activities.name: Day 2 of Antigua to Lake Atitlán
activities.description: Great day of big down and then up. Camping at 8200’ on a community soccer field.

activities.name: Day 3 of Antigua to Atitlan
activities.description: Mostly a ridge walk through a high forest. Fantastic. Includes some photos from after dark last night including volcano Fuego erupting. Yep… amazing Guatemala.

activities.name: Antigua to Atitlan: day 4, the final day
activities.description: Down to a coffee finca, a hanging bridge and one last ridge climb before a 2000’ descent to our goal. Read about it: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lake_Atitl%C3%A1n (photos seem to be in a random order)

activities.name: Short leg stretcher
activities.description: First run in 9 days… felt pretty good!

activities.name: Linking lakes with lots of stops
Standard Tangle
activities.description: Felt pretty decent.

activities.name: Norway Hill Slip-n-slide
activities.description: Fun activity with the gang!

activities.name: Using Garmin’s “Race an activity” feature
activities.description: to go about 20s faster than Friday on this course. It’s fun, but feeds my worst instincts of watching the clock too closely.

activities.name: Full comb of Tangletown
activities.description: Was supposed to go easy… but didn’t

activities.name: Quartermaster Harbor and the Burton Peninsula
Re-measuring the backyard ultra route
activities.description: Need to add about 100 yards to this to get to the official distance

activities.name: Above Orinda
activities.description: Joining Dave A and his regular Friday afternoon hiking group, including my old grad school friend Eric. Wonderful and beautiful.

activities.name: Nimitz Road run with Grace, Gilad, and Marley
activities.description: Reliving my favorite run from 30+ years ago

activities.name: Rambling around a foggy Tilden with Dave
activities.description: Discussing many things and  even coming across a memorial to the diplomat Richard Holbrooke who did so much for peace in Bosnia in the 90s and died way too young

activities.name: Two Bridle Laps with Herve
activities.description: One easy, one moderate. Felt good!

activities.name: Up Phinney Ridge the easy way
activities.description: Felt good. One stop at the top.

activities.name: Around, over, and across from Seward Park
activities.description: With Herve, Ana, Lauren, Anita, and Mintu

activities.name: A little this, a little that
activities.description: Jog to bleachers; bleachers; push-ups; lunges; 4x200m on the track; jog home

activities.name: Coffee date with Herve
activities.description: It’s what retired people do I believe

activities.name: Frosty one way from QA
activities.description: Chris had an appointment up there. Feeling rather sluggish after Wednesday’s big effort.

activities.name: Redmond Watershed with regulars + Mattias!
activities.description: Good to have some new blood!

activities.name: Coach Nick’s workout
activities.description: Including a mile on the track

activities.name: Soggy Bottoms and Tops of Tangletown
Run like Haruki Murakami
activities.description: In his memoir he describes his running habit at 60: 6 miles in 60 minutes, 6 days a week. Simple.

activities.name: Not every run needs to be a Jordan curve
activities.description: There was a reason for those three little flourishes. Two of them were to enable me to start or end some new segments I created, and the third was to get me above 3 miles.

activities.name: Exploring Pedernales Falls Park with Chris
Wedding Day jog with the bridegroom
Little tour of Town Lake with Chris
Smooth Phinney with Steve and Tamara
Electric guitar run before breakfast
activities.description: Very pretty, but not a good idea for me to try to go that far on some gel and peanut clusters. Lots of walking.

activities.name: Tamara’s circuit training, with herself and Steve
activities.description: Stair reps, dips, planks, hill sprints, side shuffles, abs work, and a few other things I can’t quite remember

activities.name: Glorious spring day on the Nordic trails at Sun Valley
Greenhorn Gulch with Luna and Skipper
activities.description: And Jeff S who showed me his backyard. Spectacular.

activities.name: Solo classic ski in spring slush
activities.description: Good workout!

activities.name: High times on the Harriman trail
activities.description: Classic skiing with Chris. The Big Wood River valley is hard to beat

activities.name: Observed by two coyotes
activities.description: Luna was off leash and chased them. Oh oh! Luckily she gave up when they went over the ridge. Leashed up we proceeded around the corner… they sat and watched us go by.

activities.name: First run in a week
activities.description: Opposite of smooth

activities.name: Arboretum and Montlake Construction Inspection
activities.description: With Steve, Anita and Mintu

activities.name: Another Haruki Murakami run
activities.description: Trying to maintain a super-even pace over the ups and downs was fun

activities.name: Bit of a slog
activities.description: If not for Tamara and Steve I would have walked a lot more. Thank you!

activities.name: How to make a stegosaurus
activities.description: Spring is coming to Tiger Mountain.

activities.name: Little Cougar loop with Mattias, Tamara, and Mintu
Short and sweet on my little home hill
Solo 13 on the Burke Gilman
activities.description: Murakami-steady on the way out; Pace ladder on the way back. Felt pretty good — at least until I was close to done

activities.name: Easy all the way
activities.description: Started the run with Lauren

activities.name: Fastest “tangle” in a year
activities.description: Not exactly sure why!

activities.name: Grand Ridge Lollipop with Monica
activities.description: Very fun to run together for the first time. There  will be more!

activities.name: Crown of Magnolia with Priya, Anita, Mintu, Tamara, and Mattias!
activities.description: Glorious cool sunny morning, and great views out to the sound

activities.name: Breathing only through the nose
activities.description: Herve swears by it. As expected I found the first attempt very tough, and am still feeling a little O2 deprived 30 mins later!

activities.name: Graduate level Jordan curve in the arboretum
Fresh snow on Rattlesnake Ridge
activities.description: Mark and I and hopefully Herve are doing the Rattlesnake trail 1/2 marathon on the 16th. We went up for a look at the trail and brought Steve along too. Glorious weather and great fun.

activities.name: Recovery shuffle to start… better later
Short run or enormous SIM card?
Another great Bridle Trails run with the gang
activities.description: Anita, Mintu, Priya, Mattias, Herve, Ana, and Lauren today.

activities.name: Maximal Jordan curve in Tangletown?
activities.description: Took nearly as long to plan it as run it.

activities.name: Little one with an uphill push
Trail work for the WTA
activities.description: Filling low spots with gravel and building rock retainers. Quite fun after the snow stopped falling!

activities.name: A little bit bonky
activities.description: Lots of stops

activities.name: Easy on the knee
activities.description: I’m off running for a little while… right knee started feeling weird last week, so trying to not make things worse. Herkimer coffee makes everything better

activities.name: Half saunter half shuffle in the sunshine
Surveying the Middle Fork Trail and the Dingford Creek road
activities.description:
This part of the MFT was mostly rocky and muddy right now, so not as runnable (for me) as I had been hoping. The Road to Dingford Creek has some very big puddles of unknown depth. Unclear whether a low-medium clearance car could make it in the summer.
Very glad to get way into the Cascades on Earth Day though!

activities.name: Kneeded that
activities.description: But didn’t kneed any more.

activities.name: Springtime in St Edwards
activities.description: Trees, canyons, ridges and Lake Washington. What more do you need?

activities.name: Hills and Half Mile intervals
activities.description: A couple of each

activities.name: 4 weeks to David’s Backyard Ultra!
activities.description:
18 people are signed up for my informal endurance event on this course on May 28th. Are you? 

Don’t be put off by the word “ultra” — one or two laps is great — come join the fun, and score a one of a kind Welsh slate medal!

activities.name: Madison Park and the Arboretum with Lauren, Anita, and Mintu
Ebey’s Landing with my sweetie
activities.description: Is there a more beautiful overlook of Puget Sound?

activities.name: Ran out of juice at 3 miles
activities.description: Lauren continued!

activities.name: Top end of the CCC road + Garfield Ledges with Steve
activities.description: How green is our valley? Glorious stroll/jog/catch-up with Steve. A little detour to show him the elegant footbridge across the Middle Fork.

activities.name: Soggy forest bathing with Mark
activities.description: It’s mud season at Bridle Trails

activities.name: Clyde Hill and Medina with Anita and Mattias
activities.description: Discovered a public dock in Fairweather Bay that needs to be jumped off when the lake is a little warmer!

activities.name: It’s hard to run on the flat by yourself
activities.description: There are no veggies of uphills or desert of downhills. I always end up going a bit fast and having to walk.

activities.name: Comb + perimeter
activities.description: Getting ready to see if I can’t get 10 Jordan curve miles in my little neighborhood

activities.name: The missing jigsaw piece
activities.description: Next step is to put this curve together with yesterdays.

activities.name: Taking it slow on Cougar
activities.description: Enjoyed exploring some southern trails that I hadn’t been on before

activities.name: To and from Retreat to meet a mate
Capitol HILLs with a big gang
activities.description: Thank you Anita, Steve, Ana, Herve, Tamara and Lauren, with Chris, Mintu and Steve walking

activities.name: Fine tuning the lap
activities.description: Previous versions have been 5/100s of a mile too short. This one is 1/100th too long. I think I’m overthinking this!

activities.name: Beautiful Des Moines Creek Trail
activities.description: Worth a visit, especially before or after an airport run for Seattle people.

activities.name: Best Jordan Curve ever
activities.description: If you can’t run fast, run far. If you can’t run fast or far, invent other measures that you can be the best at.

activities.name: Adding some new tricks to the coyote-raven loop
Luscious green lollipop with Anita, Lauren, Tamara, Grace, Evan, and Mintu
Easy Sunday run to avoid having to join the group workout
Big Molar Run
activities.description: First time in six years! Went slow, but still had to stop often. Strangely exhausting.

activities.name: Short and sun-stop-y
activities.description: But fast between the stops

activities.name: Standard tangle under high humidity. Oofta!
activities.description: I am such a delicate flower.

activities.name: Marking the tricky bit of the course
Lap #1 of my backyard ultra
activities.description: Showing people the course

activities.name: The real reason to attend Nick’s workouts
Chasing segment goals
activities.description: Coach Herve helped me set a new PR for downhill through the canyon. After a few minutes rest we came back up in my fastest time in 3+ years. Felt better than I expected! Then coffee at Herkimer of course.

activities.name: A bit too warm for me
activities.description: Ran with Lauren and Tamara… I cut it short because 60F and sunny doesn’t work too well with this delicate flower

activities.name: Reverse Dayton
activities.description: Nice and cool this morning

activities.name: Trees and water loop with Monica, Melanie, Lauren, Landen, Tamara, and Herve.
activities.description: Fun run… Great group!

activities.name: Little leg stretcher before the beginning of my long camping road trip
activities.description: Hopefully lots of outdoor adventures to follow

activities.name: Palouse Baby!
activities.description: Farm roads of the dry land wheat. Day 2 of my roadtrip

activities.name: The Frenchglen experience
activities.description:
If you find yourself in lonely south eastern Oregon, I can recommend the Frenchglen hotel, which still operates as it must have 100 years ago.

Its on the edge of the Malheur National Wildlife Refuge — a high desert valley watered by runoff from the western slopes of Steen’s mountain.

activities.name: Exploring around the Page Springs campground
activities.description:
👏 1.02 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Michael Samples was right
activities.description:
What you need around Steens Mountain is a gravel bike. Steens Mountain loop road is 50+ miles of good gravel and amazing scenery topping out at 9000’. A bit too much for these legs. Photos from my drive on the parts of the road that are open (whole loop by July 1st apparently)　
👏 1.25 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Rubicon Trail at Lake Tahoe
activities.description: It claims to be 15 miles round trip and it sure felt like that. Went very slow but the altitude made this a tough one. Stunningly beautiful though!

activities.name: Maggie’s Peak overlooking Lake Tahoe
activities.description: Hiked up, jogged down. Glorious, and trailhead was only 5 minutes from my campsite and a 2nd breakfast to follow. That’s living.

activities.name: Return to Matterhorn Peak
activities.description: About 10 years ago [strava://athletes/4970596/14]David Anderson accommodated my strange desire to find the campsite of Gary Snyder and Jack Kerouac when Gary introduced Jack to the outdoors and climbing in the late 50s. It is described in detail in Kerouac’s “Dharma Bums”. It’s a bus-sized overhanging boulder that Snyder knew about and had camped under before. I returned here, and also found the spot where they apparently sat and meditated while waiting for their friend who was hours behind and didn’t make it up until the next morning.

activities.name: Sneaking up on Half Dome at sunset
activities.description: Many people go to Glacier Point for the sunset view of Half Dome, but if you are on CA 120 I can recommend the very easy hike out to Mt. Watkins which puts you even closer to Half Dome than Glacier Point. It’s totally runnable as long as your lungs are good for running at 8,500’. Mine are not!

activities.name: Modest in Modesto
activities.description: Down by the creek. Paved trail out, unofficial bike trail back. While the clothes were washing at the laundromat. #roadlife

activities.name: Father’s Day hike with Grace and Gilad
activities.description: Matt Davis trail to Steep Ravine to Dipsea. Lovely weather and company!

activities.name: Canyon Creek Trail in the Trinity Alps
activities.description:
With Dave and Ron. Great trail… but damned long. I had dreams of running the smooth downhills, but after 15 miles, nah-ah.

And thank you Strava for adding video support!

activities.name: Rafting on the Trinity with Ron and Dave
activities.description: Yes it was very flat, but we had a few moments of excitement and I swam a couple of times. 100F when we got out of the water!

activities.name: Amazing mountain pool, 1/2 mile from the car
activities.description: With Ron and Dave. Some scrambling was required to get to the this pool which is above the main waterfall that most people visit. Only turned Strava on for the return stroll.

activities.name: Great run with a big crew in the watershed
activities.description: Followed by home made and home grown strawberry rhubarb pie at [strava://athletes/1826934/20]Sergiy Matusevych 🇺🇦 and Natalia’s place. Mmmm good!

activities.name: Streets and canyon with Ana and Herve
Snake by the lake
activities.description:
Sticking to the shady side of the streets　
0.71 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Sculpture and Sound
activities.description: With Ana, Herve, Priya, Mintu, Anita, Mattias, Tamara, and Terri

activities.name: 1 more mile
activities.description: To make up for a watch malfunction this morning

activities.name: Run to Pie
activities.description: Arriving at workout nice and late… but still scored a piece of Nick’s excellent cherry pie

activities.name: Good for 6… dragged for 2
activities.description:
👏 4.09 new miles

activities.name: A whole lot of Bridle Trails with Herve
Standard Tangle on a humid morning
One social lap; one anti-social one
activities.description: Meaning that I encouraged Herve to pull us along at a speed where nobody could chat on the 2nd lap

activities.name: It’s Bear Grass Season on Granite Mountain
activities.description: With Steve. He’s the partner you want when you want to start early

activities.name: Short and sunny
activities.description: Feeling sluggish after Granite Mountain yesterday. Still thinking about back light on the bear grass early yesterday morning.

activities.name: Short and sweet at Saint Edwards
activities.description: With Lauren, Mattias, and Rami. Chris and Mary walking.

From Bill’s place. Quiet Sunday morning on Vashon

Very fun to chat and run with him

activities.description:
Glorious day mostly in the shade, with two spectacular water stops. My GPS went a bit wonky near the end. More like 16 miles than 17.4　
🎷🐛 7.56 new miles

-- From Wandrer.earth

activities.name: Tamara’s Lollipop with Herself and Lauren
activities.description: Still sluggish after Tuesday; they dragged me along nicely

Tricky and rocky — at least for me. One fall, but luckily into bushes not rocks.

Deception Pass State park with Chris, Grace and Gilad

activities.name: Short and Sweet under Cool and Cloudy
Snoopy flies again
activities.description: Presumably looking for that Lost Beagle they named the trail after. With good natured Herve who didn’t complain (much) about the start time. Glorious!

activities.description:
Hitting the Madrona and Leschi stairs after dinner. With Chris.

activities.name: Mellow 6 with Mintu, Anita, Mattias and Lauren
activities.description: With Chris and Mary doing their own route. First run in new trail shoes. I was feeling frisky!

activities.name: Phinney before if gets too phot!
activities.description: Still stopped a couple of times to cool down

activities.name: Two hills and six miles with Tam
activities.description: Very fun if a bit sunny

activities.name: It’s cooler in Carkeek
activities.description: West facing canyons are where to find shade in the morning

activities.name: Short and solid
activities.description: In the warm morning

activities.name: I’m sure there is a lake around here somewhere
Snoqualmie tunnel, then over the top
Ultra Trail de Mount Phinney
activities.description: It’s thousands of inches high.

activities.name: North by the ridge; south by the Burke Gilman
activities.description: Fun outing with a big group: Ana, Herve, Anita, Lauren, Tamara, Kimi, Landen, and Melanie!

activities.name: Short and shady
activities.description: and somewhat speedy

activities.name: Boys will be boys: Blistering pace at Bridle Trails on a warm morning
activities.description: With Mintu and Herve. My watch under-reported the distance -- pace was about 9:35 according to Herve's watch which is fast for me on these trails.

activities.name: Walking like Edward Payson Weston
activities.description: In 1911 he walked across the country at age 71 mostly at the cracking pace of 4 mph. He had bet that he could do it in 100 days. It took him 104 days, and he was so annoyed that after a break in California he turned around and walked back in less than 90 days.

activities.name: Down the hill; up the hill
activities.description: on a warm morning. Nice to run past Tamara and Nick playing pickleball with intensity!

activities.name: It must be the new pink shorts
activities.description: For some reason they were on sale at REI. They were waiting for me.

activities.name: Lower Tiger + Nook Loop + Issy-Pres Trail
activities.description: With Lauren, Monica, Mattias, Landen, and Melanie. Great crew, great run!

activities.name: North Cascades Classic #1: Sahale Arm
activities.description: Steve T and I did a two day camping trip without campsite 1/2 way between Sahale Arm and Hidden Lake Lookout trailheads. Two days of pure North Cascades magic. Felt like we were in Switzerland!

activities.name: North Cascades Classic #2: Hidden Lake Lookout
activities.description: With Steve T

activities.name: Which way the whale?
activities.description: Track credit to [strava://athletes/60177623/12]John Skovron.

activities.name: Rare sighting of the Forest Yeti at the Middle Fork 1/2 marathon
activities.description: Thank you nwtrailruns.com for another great event. Pace corrected for distance was 12:45/mile which is pretty good for me on rocky/rooty single track. Turned my ankle (twice!). There will be some hobbling tomorrow.

activities.name: Three bumps on a log
activities.description: Baby-ing my ankle. Should be fine by tomorrow!

activities.name: That loop really is too short for the backyard ultra
activities.description: Need to work out the most elegant way to add about 150-200 yards to it

activities.name: Slice of bread that got wet
activities.description: With numerous cool-down pauses

activities.name: Caption Contest Run
activities.description: What do you see?

activities.name: Bridle Trails with the gang
activities.description: Steve, Tamara, Kim, Melanie and Monica! Great group, but no photo. Ouch!

activities.name: Mountains beyond mountains?
activities.description: Except the top of the mountains is the bottom of our local hill. So be it

activities.name: Interlaken to a breakfast with RR at Macrina
activities.description: Using my new credit-card sized tripod. It is a miracle of clever mechanical engineering. Most fun I’ve had for $35.

activities.name: Emerald Pools in Zion, with the gang
Canyon Overlook hike with the gang
Angels Landing Hike with Ana, Herve, Lauren, Anita, and Mintu
activities.description: GPS didn’t like the canyon we had to go through to get near the top. In reality about 5 miles and 2500’

activities.name: Virgin River Narrows with a gang of 11
activities.description: It was maybe 3-4 miles but GPSes don’t really work in canyons like these. Very fun slogging up this shallow silty river

activities.name: The Zion Nobody Visits
activities.description: After the crowds of the Narrows yesterday it was great to get away to the Kolob region. And check out the impromptu performance we witnessed at the Amazing Double Arch

activities.name: Helping Evan with his long run
Fast Phinney, but with a few stops
Not the most elegant path
activities.description: Which makes me pose the following problem to the mathematically inclined: is it possible to draw a smooth closed curve in the plane that intersects itself an infinite number of times? I have no idea, but would love to see one if it’s possible.

activities.name: Run-lunch-walk from the waterfront to home
activities.description: Sculpture Park and waterfront with Chris, Steve, and Liz first, then amble home with a slice of pizza at PCC on the way. Nice way to spend a Sunday midday

activities.name: Good morning Mount Desert Island
activities.description: A little rain overnight, but the sun should appear this afternoon.

activities.name: Jordan Pond at sunset
activities.description: Glorious!

activities.name: Biking the carriage roads of Acadia
Beside the Union River in Ellsworth
activities.description: Pretty much my favorite thing to do is to use running shoes to explore a town I know nothing about.

activities.name: Above the Connecticut River with Ron, Bobby, and Chris
activities.description: Preceded by an amazing dinner last night in their 300 year old house. We are living well!

activities.name: Beech Forests of Bedford Hills
activities.description: And John Blair with home-raised eggs

activities.name: Exploring St Michaels
activities.description: By bike yesterday and this run this morning

activities.name: Beam me up, Storm King
activities.description: If you are anywhere near the Hudson Valley don’t miss the Storm King Art Center. Completely compelling. With Chris, Dawn, and Jim

activities.name: Stroll along the Kingston rail trail beside the mighty Hudson
Towards Mohonk Mountain
activities.description: The Mohonk Mountain House wanted $35 per person to hike on their trails! We said no, and found a preserve close by. Right to roam!

activities.name: Solo run to check the lungs
activities.description: Not as bad as I thought. Apologies to my friends for not joining them today but they would have been exposed to lots of coughing and spluttering, so everyone was better off. And yes, I’m testing negative for covid, so I think this is more of a seasonal cold/flu

activities.name: 1/2 a mile is it right now
activities.description: Still coughing like a…. person in the midst of a lingering chest infection. Will try for a mile next time

activities.name: Inch by inch, little snail. Climb Mt Fuji!
activities.description:
(Kobayashi Issa, 1880s)
My go-to haiku when I need to celebrate small steps.

activities.name: Every run is a good run
More miles and less coughing
activities.description: Each run since 1/2 mile on Saturday has been better than the last

activities.name: Monotonically improving
activities.description: That’s the way, uh-huh uh-huh, I like it

activities.name: Jog with Herve hearing about his Ironman
Lake Union with Mintu Anita and Steve
Grand plans in Carkeek
activities.description: Was going to try to run 3 laps, but called it after 1. Nice and blustery on the sound.

activities.name: Redwoods of Russian Gulch
activities.description: With Chris, Grace, and Gilad

activities.name: Spring Ranch with Chris
activities.description: Another bluebird morning in Mendocino. We are so lucky!

activities.name: Cold morning on the Big River haul road
Adventures in Nose Breathing
activities.description: After reading the James Nestor book. Tough and slow. Felt like I was starved for air for an hour. Apparently it’s good for me, right [strava://athletes/8506527/14]Herve Jamrozik?

activities.name: Frosty on the SVT
activities.description: When I run by myself I always overestimate the pace I can go and end up stopping a few times. Always better to run with friends!

activities.description: Ate way too much of it last night

activities.description: I’m thankful for good friends and legs that still work pretty well

activities.name: Not sure what this shape is
activities.description: Not a bad effort given I had a covid booster and a flu shot yesterday. Ready for the holidays!

activities.name: You want steep?
activities.description: Go north on Phinney Ave from Fremont. 4 blocks of hiking for me. Would like to see [strava://athletes/8506527/14]Herve Jamrozik run up it sometime.

activities.name: The less hair you have the easier it is to comb

activities.name: 8 like a metronome

activities.name: Standard tangle in the middle of a very high mileage week
activities.description: Trying to hit my yearly goal by New Years Eve. After today I’ve got 21 miles to go and 3 days. I think I can do it.

activities.name: Running with friends makes the miles go by easily

activities.name: Final run of the year, and one of the best!
